entry.title: MRM Blog 1: Setting Up SAP Market Rates Management (MRM) in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
entry.content:
Header

This blog is part of our series Automating Exchange Rates in SAP with Market Rates Management.

Explore the full series to learn how to streamline exchange rate management across your SAP ecosystem:

  • Intro: Automating Exchange Rates in SAP with Market Rates Management.
  • Blog 1: Setting Up SAP Market Rates Management in BTP
  • Blog 2: Building an Integration Flow with SAP Integration Suite
  • Blog 3: Configuring Exchange Rate Updates in SAP Public Cloud.

 

Introduction

Managing exchange rates is a critical part of any global business. SAP Market Rates Management (MRM) simplifies this process by providing automated updates for market data like exchange rates. In this blog, we’ll guide you through the detailed setup of MRM in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

 

What is SAP Market Rates Management?

SAP MRM is a cloud-based service that enables organizations to:

  • Retrieve market data such as exchange rates, interest rates, and indices.
  • Integrate this data into SAP S/4HANA Cloud or other SAP systems.
  • Use standardized providers like Refinitiv or custom upload options (BYOR - Bring Your Own Rates).

Prerequisites

Before setting up MRM, ensure you have the following:

  1. Access to BTP Cockpit: Admin rights to create and configure services in your BTP subaccount.
  2. Entitlement for Market Rates Management:
    • Navigate to Entitlements in your BTP subaccount.
    • Ensure the Market Rates Management - Basic or Advanced plan is available. If not, add it.

Setting Up SAP Market Rates Management

Follow these steps to configure MRM in BTP:

Step 1: Navigate to Your Subaccount

  1. Log in to the SAP BTP Cockpit.
  2. Select your subaccount where you want to configure MRM.

 Step 2: Enable Entitlements

  1. Go to the Entitlements tab in your subaccount.
  2. Click Configure Entitlements.
  3. Add the Market Rates Management service:
    • Choose the appropriate plan (Basic or Advanced).
    • Save your changes.

Step 3: Create a Service Instance

  1. Navigate to Services > Instances and Subscriptions in your subaccount.
  2. Click Create to add a new service instance.
  3. Select Market Rates Management as the service.
  4. Choose the plan:
    • Basic Plan: Fetch exchange rates from predefined providers (e.g., ECB).
    • Advanced Plan: Supports additional features like historical data and advanced configurations.
  5. Assign a name to the instance and confirm.

Step 4: Generate Service Keys

  1. After the instance is created, click on the instance name.
  2. Select Create Service Key.
  3. Provide a meaningful name for the service key and generate it.
  4. Download or copy the service key JSON. It contains credentials like the API endpoint, client ID, and secret, which will be needed for integration.
    • We will need following information:
      • url – URL for OAuth authentication
      • clientid – username of API user
      • clientsecret – password of API user
      • downloadUrl – URL for download market rates information
      • uploadUrl – URL for upload own market rates information

Step 5: Test Connectivity

  1. Use the credentials from the service key to test the connection:
    • Open a tool like Postman or your integration platform.
    • Send a test request to the API endpoint provided in the service key.
  2. Verify that exchange rate data is successfully fetched.

 POST: <url>/oauth/token

POST: <downloadUrl>

 

Summary

Now that you’ve set up MRM in BTP, you’re ready to integrate it with your systems. In the next blog, we’ll cover how to build an iFlow in SAP Integration Suite to fetch and distribute exchange rates.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
Hello, what credentials should I use to connect directly to HDB using HANA Tools for Eclipse?

Thanks,

-Leon

entry.title: MRM Intro: Automatic update of exchange rates in SAP Public Cloud with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, maintaining up-to-date exchange rates is essential for accurate financial transactions and reporting. The system offers several methods to manage exchange rates, ranging from manual entry to full automation:

1. Manual Maintenance:

  • Currency Exchange Rates App: This app allows you to manually enter and maintain exchange rates for each currency pair individually. It's suitable for organizations with minimal currency pairs or infrequent rate changes.

2. File Upload:

  • Import Foreign Exchange Rates App: For bulk updates, you can prepare an Excel file with the required exchange rates and upload it using this app. This method is more efficient than manual entry when dealing with multiple currency pairs.

3. Automated Updates via SAP Market Rates Management (MRM):

  • Integration with External Data Providers: SAP offers the Market Rates Management service, which can automatically fetch exchange rates from providers like Refinitiv or the European Central Bank (ECB). This service ensures that your system is consistently updated with the latest rates without manual intervention.
  • Bring Your Own Rates (BYOR): If you have a preferred data source, the BYOR option allows you to upload your own exchange rates into the MRM service, which then distributes them to your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.

This blog series provides a comprehensive guide to automating exchange rate updates across your SAP landscape. By leveraging BTP, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Public Cloud, your organization can streamline financial operations and ensure accuracy in currency management.

 

 

Blog series:

  • Blog 1: Setting Up SAP Market Rates Management in BTP
  • Blog 2: Building an Integration Flow with SAP Integration Suite
  • Blog 3: Configuring Exchange Rate Updates in SAP Public Cloud

 

Footnote

When I set this up a year ago, there was no comprehensive material available that covered the complete setup of automatic integration for exchange rates in SAP Public Cloud using the API and SAP Integration Suite. At that time, I had to navigate multiple fragmented resources to piece together a working solution.

Currently, several blogs and resources have emerged that cover parts of this process. However, I still believe there is value in presenting a fully documented, end-to-end guide. This blog series aims to describe the complete setup of the automatic integration we have successfully prepared and implemented, making it easier for others to replicate the process without the trial-and-error I experienced.

Whether you're setting this up for the first time or refining your existing configuration, I hope this guide proves to be a valuable resource.

entry.title: Data Robot and SAP: Use Case Deep Dive
entry.content:
In our previous blog, we introduced the SAP and DataRobot partnership, along with an updated solution architecture and roadmap. In this post, we will dive deeper into how SAP Datasphere and DataRobot collaborate in a real business scenario, highlighting the potential outcomes of this integration.

We will look at a retail inventory management case, where understanding inventory is crucial to avoid stockouts. This becomes challenging at scale, especially for large companies managing thousands of SKUs across multiple countries with varying purchasing patterns, holidays, and seasonal factors. Each store's performance also varies, adding complexity. The main challenge is handling this vast data scale while ensuring fast and accurate forecasts. Additionally, companies need to maintain forecast reliability over time, quickly identifying and addressing issues like data inaccuracies or performance drops before they affect operations.

For this demonstration, we are using SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud business content, which includes pre-configured information models and templates designed to simplify the development of analytics and reporting for specific business scenarios. For SAP Datasphere, this involves predefined models, ready-to-use configurations, and templates that showcase various features. These content packages are tailored to specific industries or lines of business (LoB). Similarly, SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) offers predefined stories, dashboards, and data models built around existing SAP data sources, also customized for specific industries and LoBs.

For this walk-through, we will use a dataset provided by SAP, which we will upload to an SAP Datasphere instance for reporting and modeling exercises, as shown in Figure 1. This dataset will serve as the basis for developing a forecasting model to predict sales volumes at both the organizational and monthly levels.

An analytical model is created from historical data to provide insights for business leaders. SAC will then directly consume this model from SAP Datasphere, enabling business insights to be visualized through an SAC dashboard. Figure 2 illustrates the SAC dashboard, which displays historical revenue across various regions and other factors influencing enterprise revenue.

Figure 1 (left): Data Builder in SAP Datasphere | Figure 2 (right): Sales Dashboard in SAP Analytics Cloud, based on historical data.


After reviewing historical revenue values, business leaders often ask how to forecast revenue for the upcoming year. An accurate forecast would be invaluable for enterprise planning.

To support informed decision-making, the SAC visualization dashboard can be expanded beyond basic BI reporting to incorporate predictive AI-driven sales forecasting models. While several tools are available for building such models, DataRobot stands out for its robust technical and functional capabilities in developing and deploying accurate forecasts.

In the following sections, we will explore key features of DataRobot that align with a business leader's goal of integrating accurate, timely forecasts into the decision-making process, highlighting how DataRobot addresses the predictive AI needs of SAP customers who want to rapidly build both predictive and generative AI models.

Before we dive into the steps a data scientist would take to implement the business leaders' vision, here's a brief overview of DataRobot. DataRobot offers features for both low-code and code-first data scientists, which we briefly covered in our previous blog. The following screenshot illustrates DataRobot's intuitive interface, which consolidates AI model building (Workbench), governance (Registry), and operations (Console) within a single application.

Figure 3: Home screen view in DataRobot.
The AI model creation process starts with retrieving the necessary training dataset. DataRobot provides integrated connectors to industry-standard data warehouses, including SAP Datasphere, as shown in the following screenshot. In this case, a data scientist can use the SAP Datasphere connector to access historical sales data stored in SAP Datasphere for modeling. By entering the required credentials and instance-specific information, data scientists can connect directly to the data views within SAP Datasphere used for modeling.

Figure 4: DataRobot connector for connecting to SAP Datasphere for data read/write operations.
Once the modeling dataset is acquired, the next step is typically data wrangling to prepare the data for modeling or to meet specific business requirements. DataRobot offers an intuitive data wrangling feature that allows data scientists to perform complex manipulations without extensive SQL knowledge. In the screenshot in Figure 5, the data wrangling task demonstrates the creation of new features, removal of redundant ones, and aggregation of data at both the organizational and monthly levels. The monthly aggregation was requested by the business leader to support forecasting revenue across all organizations by month for the next year.

Figure 5: Data wrangling framework enables SQL-like data modification tasks without requiring SQL knowledge.


After preparing the training dataset through data wrangling, a time series forecasting model can be developed using DataRobot's intuitive interface. DataRobot offers a wide range of algorithms – both open-source and proprietary – essential for building business-focused AI models. Supported modeling approaches include supervised and unsupervised learning, clustering, segmentation, and time series forecasting. The screenshot in Figure 6 showcases the time series model creation interface, allowing data scientists to fine-tune key aspects of the model, such as the historical data range for feature generation, the forecast horizon, and the selection of relevant accuracy measures.

Figure 6: Time series modeling interface in DataRobot.


Once the model hyperparameters are set, DataRobot automatically derives the necessary features from the original dataset to support the creation of an optimal model. It generates a model using both original and derived features, through either a fully automated or manual process. During this phase, DataRobot selects the most appropriate modeling blueprints based on the available feature sets. A blueprint represents the end-to-end process for fitting the model, including preprocessing, modeling, and post-processing steps.

After completing the modeling process, DataRobot presents a list of tested blueprints, highlighting the one that delivered the most accurate results, which is recommended for deployment. The selected model is then packaged for production deployment to generate forecasts at the desired frequency or manually if needed.

The screenshot in Figure 7 shows the leaderboard at the end of the model creation process, along with various artifacts automatically generated by DataRobot, including blueprint documentation, feature impact, and model accuracy.

Figure 7: Model leaderboard showing tested blueprints, highest-ranked model, and feature impact.
After selecting a model for deployment, users can deploy it to a production environment with a single click. DataRobot offers multiple deployment options, including one-click deployment to SAP AI Core, as shown in Figure 8.

Figure 8: Final output of the model creation process, highlighting the leaderboard and deployment options.
Once the selected model is deployed in the production environment, DataRobot provides a robust framework for monitoring its performance and maintaining prediction accuracy over time. Through this framework, users can assess data drift, prediction accuracy, and overall deployment health, as shown in Figure 9.

Figure 9: Interface for monitoring model health and data drift during operation in the business environment.
Figure 10 shows how a scoring dataset can be accessed directly from an SAP Datasphere data view to generate forecasts. The generated predictions can also be written back into SAP Datasphere for further use within SAC.

Figure 10: Scoring dataset from SAP Datasphere consumed by DataRobot to generate and store forecasts back in SAP Datasphere.
The forecasts generated by DataRobot are integrated into an SAP Datasphere data flow, becoming part of an SAP Datasphere analytical model. These forecasts can then be accessed by SAP Analytics Cloud, enabling visualizations that combine forecasted and historical sales data. For this tutorial, the generated forecasts are available in an SAP Datasphere data view, as shown in Figure 11.

Once the forecast data is generated in DataRobot and integrated into an SAP Datasphere analytical model, it can be accessed by SAC for visualization via SAP Datasphere connectivity. The forecast page, added to the original SAC dashboard, is shown in Figure 12. This new dashboard page displays the generated forecasts and highlights other key factors of interest. For example, the bottom section of Figure 12 showcases features impacting the forecast, allowing users to perform ad-hoc what-if analysis using SAC's drill-down functionality.

Figure 11 (left): SAP Datasphere data view for consuming generated forecasts | Figure 12 (right): Additional page added to the existing SAP SAC BI dashboard to help business leaders understand and make decisions based on next year's sales forecast.

Summary 

In this blog, we have demonstrated how existing SAP Datasphere Business Contents can be extended to address specific business needs. DataRobot and SAP Datasphere work together to help customers unlock the full value of their business data, providing the tools necessary to manage both scale and granularity while tackling complex use cases. This integration empowers businesses to make more informed decisions, improve forecasting accuracy, and drive better outcomes.

Acknowledgments

I would like to thank Farooq Azam from DataRobot for his valuable contributions to this blog. His expertise on the SAP and DataRobot integration was instrumental in shaping the content for this blog.

Further links 

SAP and DataRobot Partnership Announcement at Data Unleashed 2023
Previous Blog Post on Partnership Updates in 2024
DataRobot
DataRobot Resource Library

entry.title: Re: How to assign a new company code to several G/L accounts in batch?
entry.content: This process worked for me! Thanks.

entry.title: Report Request: Vacant Positions Due to Employee Departures
entry.content:
Good evening, everyone,

Following a requirement from the client, it was requested to create a report (if possible) that includes all vacant positions in the company, but only for roles that were previously filled by employees who have since left the organization.

In other words, we need to identify which positions the organization had and became vacant specifically due to employee departures. Positions that existed but were never occupied by any employee are not relevant for this report.

Do you have any ideas on how we could approach such a report?

Thank you!!!

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)
entry.content:
In general for cap, you can find the relevant extensions here https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/tools/cds-editors

I would also install the fiori tooling: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SAPSE.sap-ux-fiori-tools-extension-pack

entry.title: Issue: Data Not Displaying on CDS Endpoint Despite Presence in HANA Table
entry.content:
The Issue:

While working with SAP S/4HANA or SAP Cloud Platform, I encountered an issue where data in the HANA database table is not visible via the CDS endpoint, despite the data being present and accessible directly from the HANA table.


Here's a summary of the issue:

  • The data exists in the HANA table (Attached an image below).
  • A CDS view is defined to expose the data via an OData endpoint.
  • The OData service is properly registered, and the metadata is available.
  • However, when consuming the OData service, no data is returned, even though it should be.

 

 

Troubleshooting Steps We Tried:

Here are the troubleshooting steps we attempted to resolve the issue:

  1. Clearing Cache and Restarting Services
  2. Reviewing the CDS View Definition
  3. Verifying Service Registration
  4. Checking User Authorization

Investigating HANA CLI Query Results

Seeking Help from the SAP Community:

As I have encountered this issue in my own project, I would like to reach out to the SAP community for assistance. If anyone has experienced this problem or has insights on what might be causing this discrepancy, your input would be greatly appreciated.


I would love to hear from anyone in the SAP community who has encountered this issue before. Please share your insights, troubleshooting tips, or solutions to help resolve this issue. Your input will be invaluable in understanding and overcoming this challenge.

SAP Community

Node.js 

SAP HANA Cloud 

SAP HANA service for SAP BTP

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS Views best way to SUM() child association?
entry.content: How did you solve it? I have the same requirement

entry.title: Re: Communication error with the external tax system (VER...
entry.content:
Hello,

This error comes when there is a break of RFC connection between SAP and Vertex. It seems that during LSMW, multiple calls are made from SAP to Vertex and system has less resources assigned to handled such situations hence the RFC connection is failing in between. Please ask your BASIS team to analyze the SAP Vertex RFC health and increase resources if needed. 

Thanks

Avnish

entry.title: SAP BTP Cloud connector adding subaccount giving error obtaining subaccount certificate failed
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have downloaded cloud connector and now while adding BTP subaccount and datasphere sub account to Cloud connector we are getting the following error: "Obtaining sub account certificate failed. See log and trace files and in particular scc_core.trc for details. Note is given 2460641. Could you let us know what are the pre-requisites for this activity?  Could not getting anything specific from the note.

entry.title: Re: CAP Project - SQL query response limitation 1000 records
entry.content: See original post for reason.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cloud connector adding subaccount giving error obtaining subaccount certificate failed
entry.content: The ports, the firewall, and whatever else is mentioned in said note https://me.sap.com/notes/2460641

entry.title: Re: Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Master_D...
entry.content:
Could you provide more information, @AbdulHammed ?

BTP Neo or CF? Cloud Cloud Connector version, version of the deployed artifcats, 


My scenario:

Replicate Employee Master Data and Org. Assignments from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SAP ERP or SAP S4HANA - Version: 8.1.0

Replicate Organizational Objects from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SAP ERP or SAP S4HANA - Version: 13.0.0

 

There are 2 sources that can help you with your scenario in order to fix the issue that is causing the error "The request object has been recycled and is no longer associated with this facade":

 

1-The following SAP Note seems to solve the problem when using version 2.17.1, but I am also facing this issue, in the newer version 2.17.2.

3517060: SAP Cloud Connector - The Connection checker for backend mappings is not functioning correctly on cloud connector version 2.17.1.

 

2-There's also an recent opened issue in Apache Camel 3|CXF|Tomcat that is used in the cloud by SAP Integration Suite/SAP Cloud Integration

https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/CXF-9097

 

Best regards.

Ivo

entry.title: SAP BTP for developer training
entry.content:
Hi every body

How to get a hands-on training for SAP BTP for developers.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Groovy Script dynamic time calculation - Error "No such property"
entry.content: value is defined as a string value of 200, but you have attempted to address a property of minutes which does not exist.

entry.title: Reverse Goods issue - Error: Wait until updates of dependant data are completed in LE
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to reverse the goods issue in EWM (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition) to complete missing serial numbers in the warehouse task but get an error: Wait until updates of dependent data are completed in LE. Due to the data inconsistency the goods issue is completed in EWM but not in ERP. 

Background: I booked the goods issue in EWM without serial numbers but in ERP the serial numbers are needed. 

In Warehouse Monitor - Message Queue I can see my outbound delivery running on error but can't reject/reset the status there. Do you know how can I proceed to solve this issue?

I would be grateful for any advice. Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP IAG TandD application plan
entry.content:
It could be the Partner license. You can check with your AE.

Thanks

Prem

entry.title: S2S VPN requirement?
entry.content:
Hello Experts:

I have inserted two images here for my question. 

Our client is an existing SAP customer (installed base) on ECC. They are moving to RISE with SAP and are raising questions about the requirement for a S2S VPN connection - as shown here from "Customer Premise" in the upper left hand corner.

They want to know WHY it a S2S VPN is mandatory. They would prefer not to have to use one. 

This customer would prefer to explore THIS option.

The reason why they prefer this option is that they do not want to have remote users - i.e. those working from home or in their offices and already "in the cloud" - to have to activate a VPN before logging into their SAP environment on RISE. 

Why is a S2S VPN mandatory? 

Thank you in advance

Dan Magni

Daniel.Magni@Ameri100.com

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA DB Connectivity Issue
entry.content:
Hi, I also faced this issue in 2025. In this case it is necessary to update the version of the HANA Cloud client in your project. I used a relatively recent HANA client version 2.19.21  which I think might be the latest version at this time.

If using a python notebook, just write: 
%pip install hdbcli==2.19.21
 
I hope my comment will be helpful to other people who encounter this problem in the future😉

entry.title: Re: Asset Manager - unable to open login page after MDK u...
entry.content:
I downgraded to MDK version 24.4.3, where onboarding works OK.

My suspicion is, that in the newest MDK (24.7.6), SAP tried to fix the openURL() function on iOS 18 and broke the openURL() on Android.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3548442 nativescript.utilsModule.openUrl() doesn't work on iOS 18

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cloud connector adding subaccount giving error obtaining subaccount certificate failed
entry.content: Looks like this note is outdated, it says

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cloud connector adding subaccount giving error obtaining subaccount certificate failed
entry.content: It's the nite from your own post. Edited the URL because it cut off somehow

entry.title: Table Buffering in sm30
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a doubt on the below case and request to guide me the same.

1. I have created a custom table in development and TMG with some 1000 entries maintained via sm30 without 'Buffering Activated' in technical settings .

2. I have moved this table and the entries to quality system.

3. Now, I want to activate the table to be 'Buffering Activated' and fully buffered in development considering the performance

4. Can i perform the step-3 in development and capture in other transport and move to quality again.

5. OR, do i need to carry any additional steps or some adjusted ?

Kindly suggest me the same.

Buffering Activation which is planned for reference.

Regards,

Karthik S

entry.title: Re: SAP IAG TandD application plan
entry.content:
Pretty sure it's a typo or outdated. The documentation says "Depending on whether the plan is a test (Test), standard (Production), or tandd (Cloud T&D)" 

I have reported it a while ago but no response. I do however know since that IAG comes with Test and Prod only. They are meant to be used for DTA for Test, and P for Prod.

There is no sandbox or development for IAG, we couldnt buy it, and they are both updated at the same time.

entry.title: Re: Alternative for report module once it's deprecated in...
entry.content: We rely heavily on our customized reports in the Report Module, so we are apprehensive about this new in-house functionality and its limitations.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration — URL escaping hack
entry.content:
We shall agree that this is a serious and trivial bug in the standard HTTP Receiver Adapter… for what it’s worth I also mentioned it as a comment in the SAP KB article…

I love SAP BTP Integration Suite but now and then you catch how young it is.

entry.title: Re: Table Buffering in sm30
entry.content: Your proposal of capturing in a new transport will be sufficient.

entry.title: Re: Multiple Freight Agreements with Different Carriers i...
entry.content: Hi, FYI, I have now tested and your proposed solution works. Thanks.

entry.title: Remove or hide unused WBS in CJ20N
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

I would like to hide or delete some unwanted WBS (CLSD status) on CJ2N. I've tried the deletion flag but it only works on WBS that don't have any costs associated. 

I believe we can't delete those as there are costs associated, Is there a way to hide / remove the WBS with old costs associated from my CJ20N vision ? The goal is to have a clear view of our WBS list without being annoyed by tons of unused WBS.

Many thanks for your help,

Mael

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA failed cube
entry.content: Hi, thanks for the reply! I have tried this and calc column length is now 256, still the same error...

entry.title: Re: MDG Sizing issue regarding to sizing guide
entry.content: Hi, I am using SAP MDG for Customer and can i know what is the value which i should maintain in this view table V_USMD_MC_LIMIT ...Thanks.

entry.title: How to find Technical User mentioned during remote source connection step in SDI
entry.content:
Is there a way to find the technical user mentioned in remote source connection and also who created a remote source subscription in a HANA to HANA SDI replication?

We tried the "select * from M_Remote_connections"  query, but the remote_source_user_name field is blank.

entry.title: Re: Upload file from application server(AL11) to DMS
entry.content:
Hi Madan,

Even I am facing the same issue, did you find the resolution? How did you fulfilled requirement?

 

Regards,

Shadab

entry.title: Introduction
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

 

My name is Franca Ebhotemen, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I have a background in accounting and I have had the opportunity to work in the finance and supply chain industries.

 

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in the supply chain management, finance and cloud technology.

 

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because It would advance my career growth, enhance my skills and knowledge in utilising cloud technology to help impact and improve businesses.

 

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in cloud technology, business and Digital transformation.

 

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

 

Best regards,  

Franca Ebhotemen

entry.title: SAP Developer Challenge - SAP Community Escape House - UI5
entry.content:
The new year starts with a brand new SAP Developer Challenge! The Developer Advocates have created the SAP Community Escape House - a virtual escape house (consisting of multiple rooms) that you can play in your browser. Read on for all the details.

SAP Developer Challenges are all about having fun, trying new things, and learning along the way. You can also earn a badge in the SAP Community upon successful completion. For this year, all SAP Developer Challenges will happen in the SAP Community Escape House. For each challenge we will unlock a new room in the house, and they all have a specific focus topic. Completing a challenge means successfully escaping the room by solving a series of coding-related tasks. The first room we unlocked for January (see schedule below) is about UI5, more specifically about how to scaffold a UI5 application using the command line.

 

How to participate

Visit the SAP Community Escape House and enter your numeric SAP Community user ID. If you don't know your user ID, go to your SAP Community profile. You can find the numeric ID at the end of the URL.

 Once you see the SAP Community Escape House, select the UI5 room and start exploring it.

The user interface of an escape room includes the room itself as well as a terminal. You can click the objects in the room to receive tips. Like in a real (physical) escape room, you will have to try out a few things and explore the room until you receive helpful tips and eventually instructions. These tips and instructions are printed in the terminal next to the room. The terminal is also used to submit solutions to tasks. Read the instructions you receive carefully to understand how.

If you successfully escape the room (you will know when that happens), you will receive a badge in the SAP Community. We will assign the badges once the challenge closes.

 

The schedule

The UI5 room was unlocked on January 13th and will stay unlocked until the end of the year. However, the challenge closes on January 31st, so you will have to escape the room until then to qualify for a badge. Unlike the previous year, this SAP Developer Challenge does not consist of weekly sub-challenges. The next room (next challenge) will be unlocked in February.

Good luck and happy escaping!

entry.title: I encountered different net value vs. the condtype value. Kindly advise solution.
Re: The 15 Most Important Topics for the ABAP Developer of the Future!
entry.content:

Thanks for explaining the Advance ABAP topics in simple terms.

Harish

Hi ,

 

Could you please check the portal guide carefully?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_Solution_Manager/dc7924c65eef4fd2afe7812029cd9c8f/453a54d22a4c4dddb8b077c7cabf6f23.html 

 

Best regards,

Leo

SAP Support

entry.title: 回复： MRP run by MRP area
entry.content:
Hi Soma,

Yes, you can achieve this by maintaining the MRP Controller parameter when you scheudle an MRP job.

Best Regards
Matt

entry.title: 回复： Utilization In Hours in Manage Work Center Capacity
entry.content:
Hi,

This 32.55H value is the sum of all the production orders' operation time which fall on that date, so you should check the order detailed data.

Best Regards
Matt

entry.title: Re: Groovy Script dynamic time calculation - Error "No such property"
entry.content:
Thank you for response. Could you please guide me how I can pass the value dynamically.

This is working fine in Groovy compiler.

entry.title: Re: Groovy Script dynamic time calculation - Error "No such property"
entry.content:
Original statement is as below. I need to replace 30 with dynamically

DateTo = new Date() use( groovy.time.TimeCategory ) { DateFrom = DateTo - 30.minutes }

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP Community Almaty!
entry.content: Hi, Do we have Separate Localization Package for Kazakhstan? Especially if SAP has provided any solutions for Customs Imports Declaration and Virtual warehouse solutions? Please suggest

entry.title: Re: T_code webgui shows error - page can not be displayed
entry.content: Hi Satish, Thank you for reaching out to the SAP Community. May I ask if you can manually enter the correct SOLMAN_SETUP URL in the browser to access to Solution Manager Configuration page? If yes, a suggestion, you can check if the step Define HTTP Connectivity is green in Infrastructure Preparation, or if you used SAP webdispatcher here, check if the connection test is ok (Whether the SAP webdispatcher is able to redirect to the correct URL, if further help is required, you can contact BC-CST-WDP). Best regards, Leo SAP Support

entry.title: Issues Completing Tutorials with SAP Build Work Zone on Trial Account
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently working through the following tutorials:

  • SAP Build Work Zone - Add a Workspace
  • Access SAP Build Apps via SAP Work Zone
  • Work Zone Workshop - Access

However, I am using a trial account and cannot access certain features required for these tutorials. For example:

  • In the SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition, I cannot access the "Manage Workspaces and Workpages" section, which seems to be exclusive to the Advanced Edition.
  • I also noticed that the prerequisites for some tutorials mention receiving a specific URL, username, and password for the training account, which I did not receive.

My main question is:

  • How can I complete these tutorials on a trial account, or is there a way to request temporary access to the Advanced Edition for training purposes?
  • Alternatively, can I contact the tutorial instructor or support team to gain the necessary credentials or guidance?

I appreciate any advice or assistance you can provide to help me resolve this issue.

Thank you in advance!

Best regards,
Luigi Funaro

entry.title: Creating a Product Plant Planning view is not supported when updating an existed Product Master Data
entry.content: Hi Team, I tried to create a Product Plant Supply Planning view when updating a specific Product Master Data through integration api (SAP_COM_0009), but there is no available api provided. Please help to check.

entry.title: Re: Pre and Post Test logic
entry.content: thank you so much

entry.title: 回复： Engineer-to-Order Production with Variant Configurati...
entry.content:
Hi Ankit,

I also cannot see the workflow diagram from my side.
I wil check internally what's going wrong.

Best Regards
Matt

entry.title: Re: material type with effectivity is not allowed for mul...
entry.content:
You can try to add the material type into routing with SPRO, Path: Production-> Basic Data-> Routing -> General Data -> Define Material Type Assignments T-code: OP50

TL type = "*"   Material Type = "UNFG"

entry.title: Re: Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Master_Data_And_Org_Query failed
entry.content: we're also experiencing the same issue since Jan 11, 2025, 04:45:29 UTC+8

entry.title: Re: Issue: Data Not Displaying on CDS Endpoint Despite Presence in HANA Table
entry.content: Thank you for your response  @gregorw  and Yes, we are indeed facing this issue with a CAP (Cloud Application Programming) application.

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA failed cube
entry.content: @abalasescu2 - was it the same error message or different? I think you need to check if the new dimension is associated with FACT_TABLE_1_A_33_BRANCH_ID or not. The one that was pointed out in the error message is this FACT_TABLE_1_A_33_BRANCH_ID field.

entry.title: Re: Class to Create Material data - CL_MASS_SAVE_MAT_PARA...
entry.content: So your recommendation is to use ‘BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA’ and not the class right?

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA enablement issue
entry.content:
Hi @abalasescu2 - I don't have access to this environment so I cannot check.  Can you please check if you can create canvas report with EC data and see any data when viewing the report?  If the report is error, most likely the ODS report has not been setup and you need to get CS to set it up.

If the canvas report with EC data is working, rather than removing the dimension, you can try to change the dimension into manually configured without any label or description.  Basically, just bring the field from Job Info as is.  If still error, try changing the Job Info field used for these 2 dimensions.  It is possible that the customer did not setup these fields for their EC.

entry.title: How do we get SF to mark the SCORM as complete for participants who reach the end, ignoring score?
entry.content:
Hello,

We have had a vendor create a lovely SCORM elearning module in Articulate Storyline for us where participants work through the slides to learn their responsibilities before being tested on their knowledge. If they pass the knowledge questions, then they are congratulated and told they can close the module without the need to attempt it again.  If they fail, they are directed to speak with their direct manager to further clarify their responsibilities and are also told they can close the module with the need to attempt it again. So, regardless of whether they pass or fail the quiz, we do not require that user to again open and attempt the SCORM module.

However, we have been unable to work out how to deploy this in this fashion in our SuccessFactors LMS. 

How can we set this up in SuccessFactors Learning?

Recording them as complete/incomplete/failed/passed etc isn't as important to us as the SCORM module simply being removed from their learning assignments and the user receiving no more notifications to go attempt the module.

Thanks so much for your help!

entry.title: Re: HR perk Calculation ( INHRA )
entry.content: How did the issue got resolved . Can you please help ?

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hello Graziela,

After applying TR Phase I and notes through SP-Level 209 for Release 604, the Tax Form Group text in T51T1 for Box 14 Tax Groups W24A through W24J were overlaid.  The description of the text we had configured before is now generalized text Box 14 amount 1 through Box 14 amount 10.  This has not been experienced in previous years.  The text has always remained as configured and labeled per our customer requirement.  Is this a normal behavior for the text to be overlaid with current Tax Reporter notes?  

Our intent is to relabel back to how we had before unless you have an alternate suggestion or advise against doing otherwise.

Kind Regards

Debbie

entry.title: De activate (unhide) UI.HIDE feature from standard CDS view
entry.content:
In C_SUPPLIER CDS view we have BANCKIDENTIFICATION field it have has UI.HIDE annotation is active because of this we can see BANCKIDENTIFICATION field in CDS view level but not at "Display Supplier list" app level. business requirement to show this field in APP. 

Please guide me how we can show unhide or show  BANCKIDENTIFICATION field at app level.

entry.title: NexGen AI Agents for SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
NVIDIA’s Jensen Huang says that - 'IT will become the HR of AI agents'. Source

Epoch

Creating AI agents begins with defining their purpose and value. Managing their lifecycle—from development to deployment—can be complex and challenging. When it comes to SAP S/4HANA, finding a ready-made solution for AI agents is rare. However, thanks to NexGen.AI, we now have SAP AI Agents that can address some of these challenges. In this blog series, I’ll share how I built an AI agent group in 2024 that sped up SAP processes and significantly reduced manual effort.

 

The Challenge 

One of the biggest hurdles we encountered daily was dealing with unstructured emails. Automating this process wasn’t straightforward because:

  1. Emails had to be monitored, categorized, and forwarded to the right team.
  2. Team members manually extracted information from emails to process them further.
  3. This created a heavy workload, particularly for email categorization and processing file attachments.
  4. Additionally, each email needed to be acknowledged with an appropriate response.

To address these issues, we identified the need for advanced automation in pre-SAP processes. Unfortunately, the SAP ecosystem offers limited solutions that integrate SAP-specific knowledge with automation capabilities.

By leveraging NexGen AI Agents and SAP Build Process automations, we developed a solution to handle incoming emails intelligently. This AI agent could automatically sort emails, extract key information, process attachments, and send accurate responses—all with minimal human intervention.

This project demonstrated how AI agents can streamline and accelerate SAP processes, saving time and reducing effort while improving overall efficiency.

 

Is the AI-Agent already here from the Movie 'Matrix'?  

The moment has arrived, and we’ve entered a quiet tussle with AI. As humans, we just can’t resist filling up empty spaces—if we see one, we’re all over it. Remember that one person who hilariously claimed ownership of the Moon? Well, I spotted a free space in AI agent integrations with SAP business processes—and, of course, I couldn’t resist jumping in to make the most of it!

Working with AI agents is a bit like having a pet dog you adore. At first, the dog is clueless about life’s purpose but is very clear about its love for food—and you love it despite its chaos. But as any dog owner knows, with daily training and patience, even the most scatterbrained pup can learn new tricks.

AI agents are no different. Start them off with small, simple tasks so they can learn quickly and perform better. By breaking down big problems into bite-sized pieces, we can train these “AI puppies” to become specialized pros, equipped with the right tools to nail every task. Bonus: no chewed-up shoes!

Now, here’s how we’ve planned to show off these AI agents and their superpowers in automating business processes like pros!

The image above provides an overview of the high-level process flow and scope. The AI agents I have used or prepared are out-of-the-box (OOB) solutions from different platforms.

Application Scopes

The model integrates with:

  • Outlook
  • Azure
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • ServiceNow

Agentic Tools in Scope

The AI agent functionalities are built using:

  • Azure Logic App
  • SAP Build 
  • LangChain Framework
  • SAP AI Core - SAP Generative AI Hub

Overview of AI Agents

The process begins with an inbound email received at your business email address.

AI Agent: Email Analyzer

This agent leverages Cognitive AI and fine-tuned large language models to analyze emails and extract key details, such as:

  • Email Intent
  • Details of Email Attachments
  • OCR Processing for Attachments

AI Agent: Business Analyst

This agent is a collection of multiple mini-agents equipped with specialized tools to perform a variety of tasks. In this scenario, the tools include:

  • SAP OData AI: Extracts user-requested information and converts it into API calls.
  • SAP Workflow Tool: Creates workflows in SAP based on the inbound email.
  • ServiceNow Integration: Directly interacts with ServiceNow APIs to extract information and provide responses.
  • RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation): Connects to a knowledge base to handle user queries effectively.

AI Agent: Email Responder

This agent uses Generative AI to craft email responses. After processing inputs from previous agents, it generates a reply that is both accurate and context-aware. With the right prompts, it achieves results that surpass traditional solutions like ChatGPT, providing seamless and efficient communication.

This streamlined approach ensures end-to-end automation for business processes, making them faster and more efficient. 

Now, let’s dive into these two key components (and sprinkle in a bit of humor):

Shared Memory

This is where the magic happens. Shared memory is like the group chat for all the agents—except it’s actually productive. Think of it as the baton in a relay race: when one agent finishes its leg, it hands over the processed info to the next agent, saying, “Your turn, buddy!” With an organized shared memory template, the whole system runs smoother than a well-trained pet dog (you know, the one that finally stops chewing your shoes and starts fetching them).

Processing Logs

Now, logs might sound boring, but trust me, they’re the unsung heroes here. Imagine having a diary where every decision the LLM agent makes is recorded—complete with timestamps and all the juicy details. The best way to do this? Build a CAP application and store logs periodically. Not only does this give you confidence (“Hey, we’ve got receipts for that decision!”), but it also saves you when things go sideways. If the AI messes up, you’ve got a central “oops archive” to figure out what went wrong and fix it.

Revisiting the Steps:

1. New Email Arrival
A new email lands in your Outlook or Gmail inbox, triggering the event listener. This fires off a chain reaction to kickstart the process.

2. Email Analyzer Agent
The first stop in the chain is the Email Analyzer agent. It extracts all the relevant details from the email—like intent, attachments, or critical data—and passes this information to the next agent, the Business Analyst.

3. Business Analyst Agent
This is the MVP of the process. The Business Analyst agent takes the extracted information (a mix of structured and semi-structured input) and applies a “Chain of Thought” approach to generate outputs tailored to the input. Whether it’s interacting with your ERP systems like SAP S/4HANA or ECC, or connecting with ServiceNow and similar tools, this agent gets the job done.

4. Email Responder Agent
Finally, we reach the closing act. The Email Responder agent steps in to craft a polished response, ensuring the process ends with a well-written reply.

 

Is this the End of B2B APIs?

AI agents and event-driven systems aren’t replacing B2B APIs—they’re changing how we use them. APIs are essential for securely sharing data between systems, but their role is evolving. Instead of just being fixed tools for specific tasks, APIs now support smarter, AI-driven processes. Think of APIs as the foundation, while AI agents bring flexibility and intelligence. This shift doesn’t make APIs less important—it makes them more powerful and adaptable for today’s business needs.

What about this Business Analyst Agent?

Haha! AI agents can be a bit chaotic at times. As a real Business Analyst, it’s your job to keep an eye on them. These agents often struggle to figure out the next steps or process certain data correctly. Your role is to guide and channel their efforts to make them work effectively. The good news? These AI agents are here to handle the blunt, repetitive work, freeing you up to focus on solving critical business problems. While their responses are around 50–60% accurate, they’re a helpful starting point with your expertise leading the way!

 

feel free to share your thought..

entry.title: Re: Sap sd
entry.content:
Dear Shubham,

You can add batch or field CHARG in your condition table to meet the requirement.

Regards

Sahil

entry.title: Tcode: CKMLCP : Error Material XX Curr. type 00: Cost component sum 1,XXX.XX differs from 5XX.XX
entry.content:
Hi SAP Expert,

Please help to check the issue for CO Module 

Tcode: CKMLCP : Error Material XX Curr. type 00: Cost component sum 1,XXX.XX differs from 5XX.XX

entry.title: Sales Order as Custom Fields do not work
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I have a request to add Sales Order from Account Assignment of Purchase Requisition Items to the screen Process Purchase Requisition (V2).

I have created a custom field named YY1_SALESORDERPURRQ with business context Procurement: Purchase Requisition Item, then I created custom logic using extension point Change of Purchase Requisition Item.

May I ask if there is any problem with my code in custom logic because it doesn't do anything at all.

IF purchaserequisitionitem-YY1_SALESORDERIDEXTPRQ_PRI IS INITIAL AND
purchaserequisitionitem-YY1_SALESORDERPURRQ_PRI IS INITIAL.
    LOOP AT PURCHASEREQACCASSGNMT_TABLE INTO DATA(prqaccountassgnmt).
        IF prqaccountassgnmt-SALESDOCUMENT IS NOT INITIAL.
            purchaserequisitionitemchange-YY1_SALESORDERPURRQ_PRI = prqaccountassgnmt-SALESDOCUMENT.
        ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Hana 2.0 SP5 on SUSE Linux 15 Sp3 - support end in 31 dec 2025
entry.content:
Hi Team , 

We have HANA 2.0 sp5 running on  SUSE Linux 15 Sp3, the Linux support is ending on 31st Dec, 2025.

  1. What is the latest SUSE OS version recommended for HANA 2.00.057.
  2. How to upgrade SUSE OS ? 
  3. What are the impacts of not going for upgrade even after 31 Dec DIGITS.

Thanks


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Hana 2.0 SP5 on SUSE Linux 15 Sp3 - support end in 31...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thank you for contacting SAP Support.

Regarding to your 1st concern, could you please refer to SAP KBA 2235581 - SAP HANA: Supported Operating Systems ?
You'll be able to see that SLES 15 SP3 is the latest supporting OS version for the current HANA revision.

So, if you have a plan to upgrade OS version, then you should first upgrade HANA database to the latest SPS05(2.00.059.16) or HANA 2.0 SPS07, SPS08.
Please be informed that HANA 2.0 SPS05 will also be ended of mainstream maintenance at Dec 31, 2025.
As for this, you can take a look at SAP Note 2378962 - SAP HANA 2.0 Revision and Maintenance Strategy.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Agentic AI in SAP: Revolutionizing Business Processes
entry.content:
Introduction:

In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses across industries are seeking innovative solutions to streamline operations and enhance decision-making. One such groundbreaking technology is Agentic AI, a form of artificial intelligence designed to act autonomously and drive business value. When integrated with SAP systems, Agentic AI can transform enterprise operations by automating complex processes, reducing human intervention, and improving accuracy.

This blog explores how Agentic AI is reshaping SAP business processes, enhancing efficiency, and delivering unprecedented value.

Understanding Agentic AI

Agentic AI refers to intelligent systems that possess agency—the ability to perceive their environment, make decisions, and act independently to achieve specific goals. Unlike traditional automation, which follows predefined rules and scripts, Agentic AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and advanced analytics to:

  • Learn from historical data.
  • Predict outcomes.
  • Take proactive actions.

For enterprises running SAP, this capability opens doors to self-driven operations, significantly reducing manual intervention and operational errors.

Applications of Agentic AI in SAP

Agentic AI can revolutionize various aspects of SAP-driven enterprises by introducing a new level of automation and intelligence:

1. Intelligent Financial Operations:

  • Automating accounts payable and receivable processes.
  • Detecting anomalies in financial transactions.
  • Predicting cash flow and optimizing working capital.

2. Supply Chain Optimization:

  • Forecasting demand and automating inventory management.
  • Optimizing logistics and transportation routes in real-time.
  • Identifying supply chain risks and suggesting mitigations.

3. Human Resources Management:

  • Enhancing talent acquisition by screening resumes and predicting candidate success.
  • Automating employee engagement surveys and providing actionable insights.
  • Streamlining payroll and benefits administration.

4. Sales and Customer Relationship Management:

  • Predicting customer needs and personalizing sales strategies.
  • Automating lead generation and qualification processes.
  • Enhancing customer support with AI-powered chatbots.

5. Production and Manufacturing:

  • Monitoring equipment health and predicting maintenance needs.
  • Optimizing production schedules to minimize downtime.
  • Enhancing quality control with AI-driven defect detection.

Benefits of Agentic AI in SAP

Integrating Agentic AI into SAP systems offers numerous advantages:

  1. Increased Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks allows employees to focus on strategic initiatives, enhancing overall productivity.
  2. Improved Decision-Making: AI-driven insights enable informed decision-making, reducing reliance on intuition and guesswork.
  3. Enhanced Accuracy: By minimizing human intervention, Agentic AI reduces the likelihood of errors in critical business processes.
  4. Scalability: AI-driven processes can scale effortlessly, accommodating business growth without a proportional increase in operational costs.
  5. Cost Savings: By optimizing resource utilization and reducing manual effort, businesses can achieve significant cost savings.

Challenges and Considerations

While the benefits of Agentic AI are compelling, businesses must address certain challenges to maximize its potential:

  1. Data Quality: AI systems require high-quality data for accurate predictions. Organizations must invest in data cleansing and management.
  2. Change Management: Adopting AI-driven processes may face resistance from employees. Effective change management strategies are crucial to ensure smooth adoption.
  3. Integration Complexity: Seamless integration of Agentic AI with existing SAP systems requires careful planning and execution.
  4. Ethical Concerns: Ensuring data privacy and addressing ethical implications of AI-driven decisions are vital.

The Future of Agentic AI in SAP

The adoption of Agentic AI in SAP is still in its early stages, but the potential is immense. Future advancements in AI technology will further enhance its capabilities, enabling:

  • Hyper-Automation: Extending automation beyond repetitive tasks to more complex decision-making processes.
  • Real-Time Analytics: Providing instant insights for faster and more accurate decision-making.
  • Personalized User Experiences: Tailoring interfaces and workflows to individual user preferences and behaviors.

As SAP systems continue to evolve, Agentic AI will play a pivotal role in driving innovation and ensuring businesses stay competitive in a digital-first world.

KTern.AI: Driving Innovation in SAP Transformations

KTern.AI leverages Agentic AI to redefine SAP transformations, offering an AI-First Delivery approach that automates, optimizes, and accelerates digital transformation processes. Here’s a snapshot of its core capabilities:

  • Automated Assessments: KTern.AI automates SAP landscape assessments, providing actionable insights to optimize custom codes, business processes, and system configurations. These data-driven insights enable proactive optimization, ensuring strategic and efficient transformations.
  • Risk-Based Testing: KTern.AI prioritizes testing efforts based on risk, focusing on critical business processes while ensuring comprehensive coverage. Its generative AI-driven test case recommendations enhance efficiency, accelerating project timelines without compromising quality.

Conclusion

Agentic AI represents a paradigm shift in how businesses manage and optimize their operations. By enabling autonomous decision-making and action, it empowers organizations to achieve greater agility, efficiency, and resilience.
With KTern.AI’s innovative capabilities, businesses can harness the full potential of Agentic AI within their SAP ecosystems, paving the way for a future defined by intelligent automation and data-driven decision-making.
The journey to an AI-first enterprise begins today, and KTern.AI is your trusted partner in navigating this transformative landscape.

entry.title: SAP QM Result recording screen QE51N, with tool tip on result row or Short Text
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP QM Result recording screen QE51N, is there a way to show a tool tip on Short text like "Enter Quantitative value or Qualitative value" as required.

Regards

entry.title: PO outputs are sent to suppliers when attach any Attachment Via ME22N
entry.content: I am trying to attach an attachment through service objects icon, but this is sending the successful output message even though adding an attachment is not print relevant. Please help me understand why the output is getting sent successfully in this scenario.

entry.title: Unit of Measure related settings in SAP
entry.content:
Unit of Measure related settings in SAP

Purpose of document

This document is intended to serve as a guideline for creation of a new unit of measure and its related issues.
Introduction: Unit of Measure

Definition - Unit of Measure is the criterion based on which you measure the quantity of the material. Unit of measure is a value for a physical size.

Examples
Length dimension includes meter, centimeter and inch

Time dimension includes second, minute and hour

There are 4 different Unit of Measures used

Base Unit of Measure: This is the unit of measure in which the stocks of a material are managed. The system converts all quantities entered in other units to the base unit of measure. All material postings will occur with respect to base unit of measure.

Sales Unit of Measure: Allows a material to be sold in a unit differing from the base unit of measure. The sales unit is proposed automatically in the sales order, where it can be changed.

(Purchase)Ordering Unit of Measure: Allows a material to be purchase in a unit differing from the base unit of measure. The purchase order unit is proposed automatically in a purchase order, where it can be changed.

Unit of Issue: Unit of measure in which the material is issued from the warehouse. It allows consumption, stock transfers, transfer postings, and physical inventories to be recorded in a unit differing from the base unit of measure.
These UoM's are linked with each other for every material by conversation table which is explained below in section - IV-alternative unit of measure

 

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/mickaelquesnot_cuni-activity-7283016294755360771-v1Zz?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

entry.title: No CDS View can retrieve data of Material group in Sales org 2 view
entry.content:
Below fields existed in sales org 2 view in MM01, Which CDS view can retrieve these data?

Material group 1:MVKE-MVGR1

Material group 2:MVKE-MVGR2

Material group 3:MVKE-MVGR3

Material group 4:MVKE-MVGR4

Material group 5:MVKE-MVGR5

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: No CDS View can retrieve data of Material group in Sa...
entry.content: If you  need a CDS view to retrieve the value of MVKE-MVGR related to a specific product/sales org./distr.channel , you can use I_ProductSalesDelivery . In APP Customer data browser, you can use 'product number'/ 'sales organization'/ 'Distribution channel' as filter to find corresponding product group.

entry.title: Re: MACRS Depreciation incorrect value
entry.content: Hi Santhosh, is this really a question on the SAPUI5 UI technology? If it is rather on a business application, you might want to adjust the assigned tags?

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Spaces and Pages now listed in the SAP Fiori apps library

Hi Krishna,

Welcome to SAP world,

You can use the SAP free online course link https://open.sap.com/

Regards

SS

entry.title: Agentic AI in SAP: Revolutionizing Business Processes
entry.content:
Introduction:

In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses across industries are seeking innovative solutions to streamline operations and enhance decision-making. One such groundbreaking technology is Agentic AI, a form of artificial intelligence designed to act autonomously and drive business value. When integrated with SAP systems, Agentic AI can transform enterprise operations by automating complex processes, reducing human intervention, and improving accuracy.
This blog explores how Agentic AI is reshaping SAP business processes, enhancing efficiency, and delivering unprecedented value.

Understanding Agentic AI:

Agentic AI refers to intelligent systems that possess agency—the ability to perceive their environment, make decisions, and act independently to achieve specific goals. Unlike traditional automation, which follows predefined rules and scripts, Agentic AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and advanced analytics to:

  • Learn from historical data.
  • Predict outcomes.
  • Take proactive actions.

For enterprises running SAP, this capability opens doors to self-driven operations, significantly reducing manual intervention and operational errors.

Applications of Agentic AI in SAP

Agentic AI can revolutionize various aspects of SAP-driven enterprises by introducing a new level of automation and intelligence:

1. Intelligent Financial Operations:

  • Automating accounts payable and receivable processes.
  • Detecting anomalies in financial transactions.
  • Predicting cash flow and optimizing working capital.

2. Supply Chain Optimization:

  • Forecasting demand and automating inventory management.
  • Optimizing logistics and transportation routes in real-time.
  • Identifying supply chain risks and suggesting mitigations.

3. Human Resources Management:

  • Enhancing talent acquisition by screening resumes and predicting candidate success.
  • Automating employee engagement surveys and providing actionable insights.
  • Streamlining payroll and benefits administration.

4. Sales and Customer Relationship Management:

  • Predicting customer needs and personalizing sales strategies.
  • Automating lead generation and qualification processes.
  • Enhancing customer support with AI-powered chatbots.

5. Production and Manufacturing:

  • Monitoring equipment health and predicting maintenance needs.
  • Optimizing production schedules to minimize downtime.
  • Enhancing quality control with AI-driven defect detection.

Benefits of Agentic AI in SAP

Integrating Agentic AI into SAP systems offers numerous advantages:

  • Increased Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks allows employees to focus on strategic initiatives, enhancing overall productivity.
  • Improved Decision-Making: AI-driven insights enable informed decision-making, reducing reliance on intuition and guesswork.
  • Enhanced Accuracy: By minimizing human intervention, Agentic AI reduces the likelihood of errors in critical business processes.
  • Scalability: AI-driven processes can scale effortlessly, accommodating business growth without a proportional increase in operational costs.
  • Cost Savings: By optimizing resource utilization and reducing manual effort, businesses can achieve significant cost savings.

Challenges and Considerations

While the benefits of Agentic AI are compelling, businesses must address certain challenges to maximize its potential:

  • Data Quality: AI systems require high-quality data for accurate predictions. Organizations must invest in data cleansing and management.
  • Change Management: Adopting AI-driven processes may face resistance from employees. Effective change management strategies are crucial to ensure smooth adoption.
  • Integration Complexity: Seamless integration of Agentic AI with existing SAP systems requires careful planning and execution.
  • Ethical Concerns: Ensuring data privacy and addressing ethical implications of AI-driven decisions are vital.

The Future of Agentic AI in SAP

The adoption of Agentic AI in SAP is still in its early stages, but the potential is immense. Future advancements in AI technology will further enhance its capabilities, enabling:

  • Hyper-Automation: Extending automation beyond repetitive tasks to more complex decision-making processes.
  • Real-Time Analytics: Providing instant insights for faster and more accurate decision-making.
  • Personalized User Experiences: Tailoring interfaces and workflows to individual user preferences and behaviors.

As SAP systems continue to evolve, Agentic AI will play a pivotal role in driving innovation and ensuring businesses stay competitive in a digital-first world.

KTern.AI: Driving Innovation in SAP Transformations:

KTern.AI leverages Agentic AI to redefine SAP transformations, offering an AI-First Delivery approach that automates, optimizes, and accelerates digital transformation processes. Here’s a snapshot of its core capabilities:

  • Automated Assessments: KTern.AI automates SAP landscape assessments, providing actionable insights to optimize custom codes, business processes, and system configurations. These data-driven insights enable proactive optimization, ensuring strategic and efficient transformations.
  • Risk-Based Testing: KTern.AI prioritizes testing efforts based on risk, focusing on critical business processes while ensuring comprehensive coverage. Its generative AI-driven test case recommendations enhance efficiency, accelerating project timelines without compromising quality.

Conclusion

Agentic AI represents a paradigm shift in how businesses manage and optimize their operations. By enabling autonomous decision-making and action, it empowers organizations to achieve greater agility, efficiency, and resilience.
With KTern.AI’s innovative capabilities, businesses can harness the full potential of Agentic AI within their SAP ecosystems, paving the way for a future defined by intelligent automation and data-driven decision-making.

entry.title: Re: Restrict cost center by GL account in Ariba
entry.content: yes relation entry is the typical solution to this requirement.

entry.title: Re: Table Buffering in sm30
entry.content: You could also try first and ask a question if it doesn't work.

entry.title: Re: Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Master_D...
entry.content:
Dear Ivo,

 

these artifacts are on Neo, the versions are as follows and we’re utilizing web dispatcher not CC.

However, I have noticed a pattern that the Services are failing for every 3 calls, and the 4th attempt is succeeding and it’s repeating.

I submitted a ticket for SAP and there have been no updates so far.

  • Replicate Employee Master Data and Org. Assignments from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SAP ERP or SAP S4HANA - Version: 8.1.0
  • Replicate Organizational Objects from SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central to SAP ERP or SAP S4HANA - Version: 13.0.0

entry.title: Change Delivery Batch No & quantity.
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I want to change Outbound delivery Batch no & quantity.

Try BAPI : BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CHANGE

Quantity change successfully with this BAPI for SAME batch ( Delivery batch = BAPI input batch )

but when I was change BAPI input batch then system given error.

 

Pradip Patel.

entry.title: Re: Consumption/Adjusting the usage Cycle to PRT in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Greetings,

how are you posting confirmations on your production order? Is it per operation or per header? Is in a single confirmation of you post multiple partial confirmations?

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Staying certified without LearningHub subscription?
entry.content:
SAP revamped their certification process about a year ago, introducing the requirement to renew certifications annually through a "Stay Certified" exam. At the time, I understood that once you purchased the certification, these renewal exams would be free.

Now, with the first renewal period approaching, I’ve discovered that you apparently need a Learning Hub subscription to even access the "Stay Certified" exam. Also Learning Hub page says there's another "Partner" version of it, but I cannot access the "Stay Certified" exam through the Partner Edge.

Am I misunderstanding something here, or are these renewal exams not actually free after all?

entry.title: Re: sap.m.radiobutton circle not visible
entry.content:
We have resolved the issue be adding Visibility in the standard CSS class for the radio button.

Thank you for your help.

entry.title: Object reference not set to instance of an object error when previewing Ageing Report
entry.content:
We are using SAP Business One HANA 10.0, While we are trying to preview "Detailed Receivables Aging Report" >> Business Partner Ageing (Details) - CR (GB) (System)
it gives error Object reference not set to instance of an object.

we are looking for the solution. thank you in advance.

entry.title: for billing type - G2 (credit memo) two different types of number range reflected
entry.content:
For billing type - G2 (credit memo) two different types of number range reflected. We need only one number range for credit memo. 

Kindly check below documents for reference,

1) Billing type - G2 (credit memo) - ST40000021 

2) Billing type - G2 (credit memo) - 90000207 

Why two different number range hitted while creating credit memo?

Need root cause for the same.

entry.title: Unable to understand the few columns in IGN1 sheet while uploading GR document through DTW
entry.content:
Hello Community ,

I am going to upload few transaction of Goods Receipt document and currently using the sheet for the table IGN1, here it showing in the first column showing "parent key" and the second is showing the "line number". 

Please let me know the meaning of this 2 columns

entry.title: Re: Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Master_Data_And_Org_Query failed
entry.content:
We're also experiencing the same issue since 11/01/2025, since the automatic SAP Cloud System Updates last weekend to their Cloud Integration environments.

We are running the Neo Environment on version 7.34.10 (with no Cloud Connector)

SAP Support Incident has been raised with the software vendor.

 

Error Details:
Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Generic_Object_Query failed with message "IllegalStateException:The request object has been recycled and is no longer associated with this facade"
 
SAP Cloud System Changes:
 

From: SAP CloudSupport Alerts <sapcloudsupport@alerts.ondemand.com>
Sent: Friday, January 10, 2025 11:12:10 PM
Subject: Monthly software update

SAP Cloud Integration

Customer Communication

Monthly software update

 

Dear Customer,

Customer Name: *********

Over the course of next two days, your tenant will be updated to the latest software version.
The update will be performed with zero downtime and hence will not impact your business.
For information on the latest feature delivery, kindly refer to our official documentation:

https://help.sap.com/viewer/368c481cd6954bdfa5d0435479fd4eaf/Cloud/en-US/72ef31d2739a4b079f0d297ffa684ec8.html

entry.title: Customising fiori applications by coding
entry.content:
So I want to write add a code in existing fiori app called "Manage assignements" wherein a button is to be desabled after reading a input screen and if the input screen matches a particular value. This code is working in the console in inspect tab at runtime. 

What we want to achive is to integrate this javascript code to the fiori app itself by default. Is it possible? What efforts are required?

(function() {
    function disableButtonIfConditionMet() {
        var inputElement = document.getElementById("application-DataSetMatchingAssignment-manage-component---worklist--smartFilterbar-filterItemControl_BASIC-DISP_GROUP-inner");
        var buttonElement = document.getElementById("application-DataSetMatchingAssignment-manage-component---worklist--btnAssign");
 
        if (inputElement && buttonElement) {
            if (inputElement.value === "1520 (Trading Partner balance)") {
                buttonElement.disabled = true;
            } else {
                buttonElement.disabled = false;
            }
        }
    }
 
    // Run the function initially
    disableButtonIfConditionMet();
 
    // Set an interval to run the function every second
    setInterval(disableButtonIfConditionMet, 1000);
})();

entry.title: Re: Configure SAP Enterprise Messaging for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2020 via Service Key
entry.content:
Hello @MarioDeFelipe @syed12 ,

I have done all the given step but still not getting message in event mesh, also checked SU53 tcode no issue for authorization.

Could you please help me.

Thanks

Deepak

entry.title: Re: Restrict cost center by GL account in Ariba
entry.content: yes it does

entry.title: Cannot find BAPI in Action.
entry.content: Can Anyone tell me the solution regarding how to call BAPI

entry.title: Re: Service contract determination: Unique contract determination and automatic contract assignment
entry.content:
Hi Abhishek,

 I have actually followed your config:

1) configured the item category of the contract as per your suggestion:

2) Mainted I for SRVO:

3) Since I am using standard config, so copy control was alrfeady maintained

Here are my results:

Create Contracts:

Now Create Service Order:

 

No Service contract has been determined.

 

Could you please tell me where I am doing wrong?

regards

Sambit

entry.title: Re: for billing type - G2 (credit memo) two different typ...
entry.content:
Hello @servotechindia 

The system has determined two different number ranges to the same billing document type, likely due to flexible billing document number settings configured in the IMG Activity.

Please review SSCUI Activity: 103306 and refer to the attached screenshot for further details.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant
